summary: Linked `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_YoshiEgg.c` by making the remaining function `ftCo_800BBED4` match, then flipped the object from `NonMatching` to `Matching` in `configure.py`. The fix relied on stack/layout-sensitive C rewrites: extracting hurtbox setup into a `static inline` helper with `FORCE_PAD_STACK_*`, narrowing local scopes, replacing a manual child-null ternary with `HSD_JObjGetChild`, and moving former explicit float/string symbols to local `@NNN` symbols. The decomp.dev report recorded one new match for `ftCo_800BBED4`, improving the unit from 99.75% to 100.00%, with +732 matched code bytes and +1968 linked code bytes.
what_changed: `ftCo_800BBED4` was restructured so the unit could link: the hurtbox initialization block was extracted into `static inline void inlineA0(Fighter_GObj* gobj)`.
The new inline helper uses `FORCE_PAD_STACK_8` and `FORCE_PAD_STACK_4`, then initializes `ftHurtboxInit` and calls `ftColl_8007B0C0` and `ftColl_HurtboxInit`.
Local variable lifetimes/scopes in `ftCo_800BBED4` were tightened with nested blocks for `Fighter* fp`, `HSD_JObj* jobj`, and `Vec3 scale`.
`lb_8000C2F8` now receives `HSD_JObjGetChild(fp->x20A0_accessory)` instead of an explicit ternary on `fp->x20A0_accessory == NULL ? NULL : fp->x20A0_accessory->child`.
Manual file-scope constants and extern string declarations for `ftCo_804D8B68`, `ftCo_804D8B6C`, `ftCo_804D3B88`, `ftCo_804D3B90`, and `ftCo_804D3B98` were removed from the C file.
`placeholder.h` was added and the unused-looking `ftYoshi/ftYs_Guard.h` include was removed.
`ftCo_YoshiEgg_Anim` replaced a dummy initialized stack array with `PAD_STACK(8)`.
`symbols.txt` changed the affected `.sdata` string and `.sdata2` float symbols from global `ftCo_804D...` names to local `@210`, `@211`, `@212`, `@213`, and `@257` labels.
`splits.txt` adjusted the `ftCo_YoshiEgg.c` `.sdata` range end from `0x804D3BA0` to `0x804D3B9E`.
smart_moves: Used an inline helper to isolate a stack-sensitive hurtbox setup sequence while preserving source readability around the main Yoshi Egg capture setup function.
Used explicit stack padding macros (`FORCE_PAD_STACK_8`, `FORCE_PAD_STACK_4`, and `PAD_STACK(8)`) instead of opaque dummy data where possible, making the matching intent clearer.
Tightened variable scopes to influence register allocation and lifetime without changing the high-level behavior.
Removed artificial global data declarations once the matching build could represent those constants/strings as local `@NNN` symbols.
Updated build metadata and split/symbol maps in the same PR as the C match, so the object could be linked immediately rather than only locally matching.
decomp_lessons: When a function is very close but not fully matching, try moving a self-contained block into a `static inline` helper to affect stack frame shape and register allocation.
Prefer project stack-control macros such as `PAD_STACK` and `FORCE_PAD_STACK_*` over ad hoc dummy arrays when the goal is matching stack layout.
Nested lexical scopes can be a practical matching tactic because they constrain local lifetimes and can change generated code without semantic churn.
Once compiler-generated local constants or strings are understood, symbol-map entries should be converted from provisional global `ftCo_804D...` names to local `@NNN` labels.
A PR that links an object should include the source match, `configure.py` status flip, and any required `symbols.txt`/`splits.txt` corrections together.
Wrapper/helper functions like `HSD_JObjGetChild` may produce better matching code than hand-written null checks or direct field access, even when the behavior appears equivalent.
naming_conventions: Unidentified functions remain address-based with subsystem prefix, e.g. `ftCo_800BBED4`, `ftCo_800BBCC0`, `ftCo_800BC438`, and `ftCo_800BC3D0`.
Temporary matching-only inline helpers may use descriptive placeholder names such as `inlineA0` when no original semantic name is known.
Local compiler data symbols in `symbols.txt` are named `@NNN` and scoped `local`, replacing provisional global address-style names such as `ftCo_804D8B68` when appropriate.
Fighter common files use the `ftCo_` prefix and movement state names such as `ftCo_MS_YoshiEgg`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Extracted code into `static inline void inlineA0(Fighter_GObj* gobj)` to alter inlined code shape while keeping the call site source structured.
Inserted `FORCE_PAD_STACK_8` and `FORCE_PAD_STACK_4` before the hurtbox setup block.
Changed an initialized dummy stack array in `ftCo_YoshiEgg_Anim` into `PAD_STACK(8)`.
Introduced nested blocks around locals to influence stack/register allocation.
Moved `Vec3 scale` into the smallest scope around accessory scaling to affect variable lifetime.
Used `HSD_JObjGetChild(fp->x20A0_accessory)` in place of direct child access guarded by a ternary.
Removed file-scope constants/externs and allowed local `.sdata`/`.sdata2` entries to be emitted or represented as local labels.
Adjusted `.sdata` split end for `ftCo_YoshiEgg.c` from `0x804D3BA0` to `0x804D3B9E` to match the linked data extent.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
The decomp.dev bot reported one new match: `main/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_YoshiEgg` function `ftCo_800BBED4`, from 99.75% to 100.00%.
The bot also reported matched code +0.02% (+732 bytes) and linked code +0.05% (+1968 bytes) for GALE01.
follow_up_queries: Find other PRs that use `FORCE_PAD_STACK_8` or `FORCE_PAD_STACK_4` to complete a fighter function match.
Find other object-linking PRs that convert global `ftCo_804D...` symbols to local `@NNN` symbols.
Search for other uses of extracting code into `static inline` helpers named `inline*` as a matching tactic.
Compare `ftCo_YoshiEgg.c` with adjacent capture files such as `ftCo_CaptureYoshi.c` and `ftCo_CaptureKoopa.c` for reusable stack-layout patterns.
Search for cases where `HSD_JObjGetChild` replaces a direct null-check ternary for matching.
